summary: The user is developing a revolutionary t-shirt brand that honors their late father, Crispin Mejia Sr., who served in the U.S. Army and was passionate about Belize. The brand focuses on eco-friendly, sustainable fashion for all ages and sizes, with a commitment to donating 5% of proceeds to support homeless veterans and education in Belize.
facts: The user is creating a t-shirt brand to honor their late father, Crispin Mejia Sr., who passed away on August 6, 2020. Crispin Mejia Sr. was born in Belize, Central America, and came to America in 1968, serving in the army for 22 years and retiring as an E8 master sergeant. Crispin Mejia Sr. also worked at IBM before retiring and returning to Belize. The user’s father was married for 50 years and left behind his wife, children, and grandchildren. The t-shirt brand offers sizes ranging from baby to 5XL and targets men, women, and children. The t-shirts are made from a blend of 60% organic cotton and 40% recycled poly rPET, saving at least 4 plastic bottles from landfills. The user’s t-shirt brand donates 5% of proceeds to Stockton Shelter for the Homeless, which assists homeless veterans, and Gulisi Community Primary School in Dangriga, Belize, which provides scholarships for students. The brand is committed to sustainability and plans to release new designs and styles regularly.
